title: git-warden configuration
description: Configuration schema for git-warden (.git-warden.yml) — schema v1.2.0 (locale unified: required_language → locale)

enabled.description:
Whether to enable git-warden globally (default: true).
Used to disable all checks with a single enabled: false line for a specific repository when running as a global hook (git config --global) across all repos.
When a project config exists, the global config is ignored; on preset merge, the body value takes precedence over the preset.

preset.description:
Base configuration preset to load from a remote URL. Fetches a .git-warden.yml-format YAML from the URL and uses it as the base; local config overrides the preset.
When a project config exists, the global config is completely ignored and the order is: project config > project preset. The global config is only used when no project config exists, in which case the order is: global config > global preset.
A preset cannot specify preset.url again (nested presets not supported).

url.description: HTTP/HTTPS URL to fetch the preset config file from.

cache.description: Local caching settings for the preset fetched from the URL.

enabled.description: Whether to enable caching (default: false)

include.description:
List of conditional config inclusion rules (similar to git's [includeIf "gitdir:..."]).
Each entry's path file is merged in order as the base, then the body is merged on top.
Priority: body value > later include > earlier include.
Includes inside a remote preset config are ignored for security; includes inside included files are also ignored (no nesting). Missing files are skipped with a warning.

path.description: Path to the config file to include. '~' is expanded to the home directory; relative paths are resolved relative to the config file declaring the include.

gitdir.description:
Directory glob pattern used as the inclusion condition (git includeIf gitdir semantics). Always included when empty.
'~' is expanded to the home directory; if ending with '/', '**' is appended to match the directory and all subdirectories.

comment_language.description: Settings for checking the language used in code comments

enabled.description: Whether to enable comment language checking (default: true)

required_language.description: [deprecated — use locale from v1.2.0] Natural language required in comments (default: korean). Overridden when locale is set. Retained for backwards compatibility; convert to locale with 'git-warden migrate'.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale code that automatically sets required_language. Overrides required_language when set.

min_length.description: Minimum character count for language checking (default: 5). Comments shorter than this are not checked.

check_mode.description:
Check scope setting (default: diff).
  diff: check only comments on lines added in the staged diff (fast)
  full: check all comments in the entire staged file

languages.description: List of programming languages to parse. Takes precedence over extensions when set.

extensions.description: List of file extensions to check. Used when languages is not set.

skip_directives.description: Additional comment prefix list to skip during checking. Merged into the built-in skip list (todo, fixme, nolint, etc.).

ignore_files.description: List of file glob patterns to exclude from comment language checking. Supports '**'.

no_emoji.description:
Whether to forbid emoji in comments (default: false).
When true, reports an error if emoji are found in source code comments.

check_strings.description:
Whether to apply the same language check to string literals as to comments (default: false).
When true, required_language is also applied to string literals in source code.

skip_technical_strings.description:
When check_strings=true, skips strings identified as technical identifiers (default: true).
Skip conditions:
  - Contains a slash (/) → path or MIME type (e.g. /api/v1, application/json)
  - Pure ASCII with no lowercase letters → uppercase constant (e.g. ERR_TOKEN, MAX_SIZE)
When false, all strings are subject to language checking.

allowed_words.description:
List of English words to ignore in language checking.
The language is determined from the remaining text after removing these words from the comment.
Useful for proper nouns, technical terms, etc.

allowed_words_file.description:
Path to a text file containing allowed words, one per line.
Lines starting with # are treated as comments and ignored. Merged with allowed_words.

allowed_words_url.description:
URL to fetch the allowed words file from via HTTP/HTTPS.
Merged with allowed_words and allowed_words_file.

allowed_words_cache.description: Local caching settings for allowed words fetched from the URL.

enabled.description: Whether to enable caching (default: false)

file_languages.description: List of per-file language rules. The first matching pattern is applied (order matters).

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match against file paths. Supports '**'.

locale.description: Natural language required in files matching this pattern. Accepts BCP-47 code (ko, en, ja, zh), legacy language name (korean, english, etc.), or 'any'.

language.description: Language required in files matching this pattern. Accepts locale code (ko, en, ja, zh) and 'any'.

binary_file.description: Settings for blocking binary file (compiled executables, etc.) commits

enabled.description:
Whether to enable binary file detection (default: true).
Detects files reported as binary by git diff --staged --numstat and blocks commits.

default_policy.description:
Policy for binaries not matched by any rule (default: block).
  block: block (error)
  allow: permit (pass)
  lfs:   permit only when tracked by git LFS, otherwise block
Priority: rules (extension match) > built-in image policy (image extension → allow) > default_policy

rules.description: List of per-extension binary policy rules. The first matching rule is applied.

extensions.description: List of file extensions to apply this policy to (.png, .jpg, etc.). Case-insensitive.

ignore_files.description:
List of file glob patterns to exclude from binary file checking. Supports '**'.
Use to allow intentionally committed binaries such as images and fonts.

lint.description: Syntax checking settings for data files (YAML, JSON, XML)

enabled.description: Whether to enable lint checking (default: true)
Whether to enable YAML lint (default: true)

comment_filter.description: When true, a "# git-warden: skip-lint" comment in the file disables YAML lint for that file (default: false).

ignore_files.description: List of glob patterns to exclude from YAML lint

enabled.description: Whether to enable JSON lint (default: true)

allow_json5.description:
Whether to allow JSON5 format (default: false).
When true, allows // comments, /* */ block comments, and trailing commas.

comment_filter.description:
When true, strips // and /* */ comments from .json files before strict JSON validation (JSONC mode, default: false).
.jsonc files are always checked as JSON5 regardless of this setting.

ignore_files.description:
List of glob patterns to exclude from JSON lint.
Default excludes: package-lock.json, yarn.lock and other auto-generated files.
Overrides the default list when set.

enabled.description: Whether to enable XML lint (default: true)

ignore_files.description: List of glob patterns to exclude from XML lint

enabled.description: Whether to enable TOML lint (default: true)

ignore_files.description: List of glob patterns to exclude from TOML lint

enabled.description: Whether to enable encoding checking (default: true)

require_utf8.description:
Whether to require UTF-8 encoding (default: true).
When true, blocks commits of text files that are not UTF-8.

no_invisible_chars.description: Whether to forbid invisible Unicode characters (NBSP, ZWSP, BiDi controls, etc.) in file content (default: false).

no_ambiguous_chars.description:
Whether to forbid Unicode characters that are visually similar to ASCII but have different code points (default: false).
Detects cases such as Cyrillic А (U+0410) vs Latin A (U+0041).

locale.description: BCP-47 locale to use for ambiguous character detection (default: ko).

ignore_files.description: List of glob patterns to exclude from encoding checking

enabled.description:
Whether to enable .editorconfig checking (default: true).
Automatically skipped when no .editorconfig file is found.

ignore_files.description: List of file glob patterns to exclude from editorconfig checking. Supports '**'.

enabled.description:
Whether to enable commit message checking globally (default: true).
When false, all sub-checks including no_ai_coauthor and no_unicode_spaces are skipped.

no_ai_coauthor.description:
Whether to block Co-authored-by: trailers from AI tools (default: true).
Rejects lines matching built-in AI email patterns (Copilot, Claude, Cursor, Codeium, etc.).
Regular human co-authors are not affected.

coauthor_remove_emails.description:
Additional email addresses or glob patterns to remove on top of the built-in AI patterns.
Supports '*' wildcard (e.g. "*@myai.internal"). Case-insensitive.
Built-in AI patterns: *copilot*@*, noreply@anthropic.com, *@cursor.sh, *@codeium.com, *@tabnine.com, etc.

no_unicode_spaces.description:
Whether to forbid non-standard Unicode space characters (default: true).
Includes NBSP (U+00A0), EM SPACE (U+2003), ZWSP (U+200B), BiDi control characters, etc.
BOM (U+FEFF) is permitted as a valid encoding marker.

no_ambiguous_chars.description:
Whether to forbid Unicode characters that are visually similar to ASCII but have different code points (default: true).
Example: Cyrillic А (U+0410) vs Latin A (U+0041). Based on Gitea/VSCode Unicode data.

no_bad_runes.description: Whether to forbid invalid UTF-8 byte sequences (default: true).

no_emoji.description:
Whether to forbid emoji in commit messages (default: false).
When true, reports an error if the commit message contains emoji.

locale.description:
BCP-47 locale to use for ambiguous character detection (default: ko).
Selects the per-locale confusable character table (Gitea-based).

conventional_commit.description:
Settings for enforcing Conventional Commits format (https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
Format: <type>[(<scope>)][!]: <description>

enabled.description: Whether to enable conventional commit format checking (default: false)

types.description:
List of allowed commit types.
Default: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert

type_aliases.description:
Localised type alias mappings.
Key: localised type, value: standard type.
Example: {"feat": "feat", "fix": "fix"}
When set, localised types can also be used in commits.

locale.description:
Language to apply localised type defaults.
Uses built-in mappings when type_aliases is not set.
Supported: ko, ja, zh

require_scope.description: Whether scope is required (default: false). When true, the format feat(scope): is mandatory.

allow_merge_commits.description: Skip format checking for "Merge ..." commits (default: true)

allow_revert_commits.description: Skip format checking for "Revert ..." commits (default: true)

language_check.description: Natural language checking settings for the commit message body

enabled.description: Whether to enable commit message language checking (default: false)

locale.description:
Natural language required in the commit message body. Accepts BCP-47 code (ko, en, ja, zh) or legacy language name (korean, english, etc.).
Auto-derived from commit_message.locale when empty. Overrides required_language when set.

required_language.description: Natural language required in commit messages (default: korean)

min_length.description: Minimum character count for language checking (default: 5)

skip_prefixes.description:
List of subject line prefixes to skip language checking for.
Commits starting with these prefixes have their entire message skipped.
Default: ["Merge", "Revert", "fixup!", "squash!"]

subject_limit.description: Character limit settings for the commit message subject (first line)

enabled.description: Whether to enable subject length checking (default: false)

max_length.description: Maximum character count for the subject (default: 72)

body_line_limit.description: Character limit settings for each line of the commit message body

enabled.description: Whether to enable body line length checking (default: false)

max_length.description: Maximum character count for each body line (default: 100)

exceptions.description: Global and per-feature file exclusion pattern settings

global_ignore.description: List of file glob patterns to exclude from all checks. Supports '**'.

comment_language_ignore.description: List of file glob patterns to exclude only from comment language checking. Supports '**'.

commit_message.description: List of custom rules to apply to commit messages

diff.description: List of custom rules to apply to added lines in the staged diff

protected_paths.description:
Protected path settings that block all changes (add/modify/delete) to paths matching the glob patterns.
Stronger "complete freeze" policy than append_only. Use for locked legacy directories, auto-generated files, released migrations, etc.

enabled.description: Whether to enable protected path checking (default: false). Not checked when paths is empty.

paths.description: List of glob patterns to apply protected path rules to

append_only.description:
Append-only settings that forbid file deletion, content modification, and mid-file insertion in specific paths.
Use for directories such as DB migration directories where committed content must never be changed.

enabled.description: Whether to enable append-only checking (default: false). Not checked when paths is empty.

paths.description: List of glob patterns to apply append-only rules to

filename_order.description:
Whether to check that new file names come after existing file names.
  numeric (or empty string, default): must come after the maximum of existing files by natural (numeric) sort order
  none: disable filename order checking

cache_dir.description:
Settings for checking whether files inside build artifact/cache directories (node_modules, dist, build, target, __pycache__, etc.) are committed or staged in git.
Determines the directory type based on parent directory indicators (go.mod, package.json, etc.).

enabled.description: Whether to enable cache/build directory checking (default: true)

ignore_dirs.description:
List of directory names to exclude from checking.
Example: specify ["vendor"] for Go projects that intentionally commit vendor/.

guide.description:
Settings for per-category improvement guides printed when a check violation occurs.
Prints remediation steps (specific commands/actions) once per category after the violation list and summary.

enabled.description: Whether to enable improvement guide output (default: true)

message.description: Human-readable message to display on rule violation

required.description:
When true, the pattern must match (error on mismatch).
When false (default), the pattern must not match (error on match — forbidden rule).
